Rule Text: If the text of a card contradicts the rulebook
Source Citation: the card text takes precedence.

Rule Text: Use common sense and be cool.
The game ends when a player has vanquished all opposing Avatars
Source Citation: securing their hold on the realm.

Rule Text: To win
Source Citation: you must defeat any opposing Avatar by first reducing their life total to zero
column5: and then delivering a final death blow to the weakened Avatar.

Rule Text: If either player attempts to draw a card from an empty deck
Source Citation: they immediately lose.

Rule Text: When an Avatar’s life is reduced to 0
Source Citation: they are now at death’s door.

Rule Text: An Avatar at death’s door can no longer gain life.
When an Avatar is reduced to 0 life
Source Citation: the wounded Avatar becomes immune to damage for the rest of the current turn.

Rule Text: After the window of immunity
Source Citation: any damage dealt to the Avatar is a death blow
column4: finally severing their connection to the realm and signaling their defeat.

Rule Text: Damage dealt to an Avatar with one or more life reduces their life by that amount.
Damage dealt to an Avatar on death’s door causes a death blow.
When a minion has damage on it equal to or greater than its power
Source Citation: it dies.

Rule Text: Clear some space in the middle of the table for the realm.
Players place their Avatars on the square in the middle of their bottom row.
Players shuffle and place their atlas and spellbook decks nearby
Source Citation: leaving some space for their cemeteries.

Rule Text: Each player draws three cards from their atlas and three cards from their spellbook as a starting hand.
To take a mulligan
Source Citation: return up to three cards from your hand to the bottom of their respective decks in any order.

Rule Text: Then redraw the same number of cards you returned from each deck after a mulligan.
If you are the first player to take a turn in the game
Source Citation: skip Step 4 of the Start Phase (No Card Draw).

Rule Text: During your first Main Phase
Source Citation: your Avatar must use its ability to play a site to your Avatar’s square to establish their domain.

Rule Text: You need to choose and include exactly one Avatar card.
Your Atlas must contain at least 30 site cards.
Your Spellbook must contain at least 50 spell cards.
You may have larger decks as long as you can reasonably shuffle them.
You may include up to four copies of any ordinary cards.
You may include up to three copies of any exceptional cards.
You may include up to two copies of any elite cards.
You may include only one copy of any unique cards in a deck.
Your Avatar does not count toward rarity totals.
The realm is a 5x4 square grid where most cards are played.
When you search your atlas or spellbook for a card
Source Citation: you must shuffle it afterward.

Rule Text: If you place or reorder known cards on the bottoms or tops of decks
Source Citation: you do not shuffle it.

Rule Text: Whenever any of your cards are discarded
Source Citation: destroyed
column6: place them face-up in your cemetery.

Rule Text: Players may freely examine the contents of any cemetery at any time.
Your hand should remain hidden from your opponents.
The number of atlas and spellbook cards you hold may be observed and asked.
There is no maximum hand size.
There are four regions in the game: void
Source Citation: surface

Rule Text: Avatars can never enter a region other than the surface.
Avatars ignore many negative effects.

Any minion that somehow finds its way into the void (whether in whole or in part) is immediately banished (removed from the game) unless it has an ability that specifically allows it to exist there (such as Voidwalk).
Minions that somehow find their way to the subsurface immediately die
Source Citation: unless otherwise allowed to do so with special abilities.

Rule Text: Water sites are identified by the water threshold icon (marked with the blue wave symbol).
All other sites are considered land sites
Source Citation: including sites with no threshold icons and rubble.

Rule Text: A contiguous cluster of adjacent water sites is known as a body of water.
A contiguous cluster of adjacent land sites forms a span of land.
When a site is destroyed
Source Citation: it is placed in its owner’s cemetery

Rule Text: Rubble is a neutral land site that no player controls.
Rubble provides no mana or threshold.
A location is defined as one region in one square.
Adjacent refers to the card’s own square
Source Citation: and the squares that it shares a border with.

Rule Text: Nearby refers to the card’s own square
Source Citation: and all squares that surround it

Rule Text: Only locations that are in the same region as the referencing card (or its Spellcaster) are considered adjacent or nearby locations.
Spells and abilities can only target things within the Spellcaster’s or referencing card’s same region.
A step is the minimum unit of distance between two adjacent locations.
Players take their turns
Source Citation: one at a time
column4: following this sequence of phases: Start Phase

Rule Text: All of your cards that are tapped now untap.
All of your sites now provide their mana for this turn.
Trigger abilities that happen at the start of the turn.
Finally
Source Citation: draw a card from either your spellbook or atlas.

Rule Text: Your units can cast spells or activate one of their abilities.
You may have your units do actions as many times as you have resources
Source Citation: and in any order you like.

Rule Text: Trigger abilities that happen at the end of the turn.
Players remove all damage from their minions in the realm.
Effects that last for your turn now end.
Your turn ends.
Avatars do not heal automatically at the end of the turn.
You can only gain mana during your turn.
At the start of your turn
Source Citation: all of your sites provide one mana each

Rule Text: When one of your sites enters the realm
Source Citation: it immediately provides one mana.

Rule Text: You lose any unspent mana at the end of your turn.
If you lose a site
Source Citation: the mana it provided this turn is not removed from your mana pool.

Rule Text: If you gain control of an enemy’s site during your turn
Source Citation: it does not provide you with mana until your next turn.

Rule Text: To cast a spell
Source Citation: you must be able to pay the mana cost from your available mana.

Rule Text: You cannot pay a mana cost if the amount required exceeds the mana available.
Elemental threshold is not spent like mana.
To cast a spell or use an ability with a threshold
Source Citation: you must have at least as many matching symbols on the sites you control
column4: plus any others granted by spells or abilities.

Rule Text: All artifacts and most abilities have no threshold at all.
To cast a spell with multiple element threshold
Source Citation: you must be able to meet all of a spell’s required threshold.

Rule Text: To cast a spell
Source Citation: you need to identify a Spellcaster under your control to cast it.

Rule Text: Minions are summoned under your control atop any of your sites.
There is no limit to the number of minions that may occupy the same site.
Artifacts are conjured atop any site you control or directly into the waiting hands of one of your units.
Auras are conjured at the intersection of four squares
Source Citation: though some specify the border between two
column4: or even just a single square.

Rule Text: Magics are the only cards in the game that do not enter play when they are cast.
A magic spell goes to its owner’s cemetery as soon as it resolves.
A unit that entered the realm this turn suffers from summoning sickness until the end of turn.
A unit suffering from summoning sickness cannot tap
Source Citation: or be tapped
column4: to pay for costs associated with any ability.

Rule Text: When you summon a unit on your turn
Source Citation: it enters play untapped.

Rule Text: Monuments are an artifact subtype that cannot be carried.
Automatons are artifacts that are minions.
Automatons have the same basic abilities as regular minions and cannot be carried.
Tokens are never formally part of a player’s deck.
If a token would be placed anywhere except within the realm
Source Citation: it is instead removed from the game entirely.

Rule Text: To activate an ability
Source Citation: state your intention

Rule Text: Only the controller of a card can use its activated abilities.
Activated abilities can only be used during the main phase of their own turn when nothing else is already happening.
To tap a card
Source Citation: turn it 90° to the right from its upright position.

Rule Text: A player can only tap untapped cards.
A player can only untap tapped cards.
All of your cards in play automatically untap at the start of your turn.
Sites come into play through your Avatar’s activated ability: “Tap → Play or draw a site”.
When you play a site
Source Citation: it must be placed on void or rubble.

Rule Text: When you play a site
Source Citation: it must be placed adjacent to another site that you control or as close as possible to your Avatar if you control no sites.

Rule Text: There can only be one site per grid square.
A unit is a collective term that includes both Avatars and minions.
All units have access to five basic abilities: Move and Attack
Source Citation: Pick Up

Rule Text: Every unit can tap to activate the Move and Attack ability.
Move and Attack allows a unit to optionally move
Source Citation: then optionally attack something at its location.

Rule Text: When moving a unit
Source Citation: its journey is broken down into individual steps.

Rule Text: By default
Source Citation: units can move one step.

Rule Text: When declaring movement
Source Citation: the only restriction is that you may not repeat specific steps.

Rule Text: As you resolve each step
Source Citation: you confirm that it is a legal step at that moment.

Rule Text: You may only attack enemy units or the surface of enemy sites that are at your location.
When using Move and Attack
Source Citation: you must resolve any movement before declaring and resolving your attack.

Rule Text: If you attack an enemy unit
Source Citation: the units involved will fight.

Rule Text: To fight
Source Citation: units strike each other simultaneously.

Rule Text: When a unit strikes
Source Citation: it deals damage equal to its power.

Rule Text: Units that strike first resolve their strikes before their target can resolve theirs.
If multiple units in the same fight are able to strike first
Source Citation: they all strike at the same time.

Rule Text: If more than two units are involved in a fight
Source Citation: the controller of each side may allocate their units’ damage among the enemy as they wish.

Rule Text: Damage dealt to minions persists until the end of the turn.
A minion that accumulates damage greater than or equal to its power immediately dies and is placed in its owner’s cemetery.
Zero damage is not any damage at all.
If you attack the surface of an enemy site and your opponent doesn’t defend
Source Citation: strike the site

Rule Text: Attacking an enemy site causes the controlling Avatar to lose that much life.
Attacking a site cannot deliver a death blow.
Defend is a triggered ability used on your opponent’s turn.
When an enemy attacks another unit or site in your unit’s range of motion
Source Citation: your unit may tap to defend
column4: moving to the attack’s location and joining the fight.

Rule Text: Any number of units may defend against an attacker.
If the original target of the attack was a unit
Source Citation: the defender’s controller may decide if that unit remains in the fight

Rule Text: If the original target was a site
Source Citation: it is automatically removed.

Rule Text: Only “attacks” (caused by Move and Attack or specific card text) can be defended.
Intercept is a triggered ability used on your opponent’s turn.
When an enemy using Move and Attack finishes its movement and then chooses not to attack
Source Citation: any of your units there may tap to intercept and fight that enemy.

Rule Text: Intercepting units cannot move; they must already be at the enemy’s location.
Each unit can activate their Pick Up basic ability once per turn.
Pick Up allows a unit to pick up any number of artifacts at locations it occupies.
Each unit can activate the Drop basic ability once per turn.
Drop can only be used as long as the unit hasn’t interacted with the realm this turn.
A unit can carry any number of artifacts.
If a unit is carrying any artifacts when it leaves the realm
Source Citation: the artifacts are dropped.

Rule Text: If a unit and an artifact it is carrying stop sharing a location
Source Citation: the artifact ceases to be carried.

Rule Text: Artifacts carried by a unit are controlled by the player who controls that unit.
Artifacts that could be carried but are not currently being carried are uncontrolled.
You have ownership of all of the cards you bring to a game.
The owner of a card does not change during the game.
A card is always returned to its owner’s hand
Source Citation: shuffled into its owner’s deck
column4: or sent to its owner’s cemetery regardless of who controls the card when it leaves play.

Rule Text: When rules text uses phrases like “your minions” or “your sites
Source Citation: ” that refers to those things that you control
column4: not those things that you own.

Rule Text: A passive ability has an ongoing effect that is always active
Source Citation: as long as the card is in the relevant game zone.

Rule Text: Static effects should be applied at all times
Source Citation: even while resolving other effects or actions.

Rule Text: Static effects take precedence over triggered abilities.
A keyword is a word or phrase that functions as shorthand for a longer piece of rules text.
A triggered ability requires that a specified condition is met
Source Citation: or a specified event occurs

Rule Text: Teleportation effects move a unit directly to a specified location without taking any steps in between.
When a unit uses the Move and Attack or Defend abilities they spend no steps when moving
Source Citation: as long as the starting and ending locations of the step satisfy the moves freely condition.

Rule Text: Forced movement is not modified by a unit’s abilities.
Units that cannot move by activating their own abilities can still be moved by other cards and effects.
If an effect causes a unit to ‘take a step’ or ‘take up to X steps’ this is not forced movement
Source Citation: and the unit can modify these steps with their abilities.

Rule Text: A unit that is made to move forcibly does not tap and cannot be intercepted
Source Citation: as it is not moving as part of the Move and Attack basic ability.

Rule Text: If a site moves to another square
Source Citation: everything atop and under it moves with it (those cards are not considered to have moved).

Rule Text: A site can only move or be moved to a square without another site (a void).
A unit being carried may still cast spells or activate abilities as normal.
If the carried unit moves to a location that the carrying unit doesn’t occupy
Source Citation: the unit ceases to be carried.

Rule Text: Units normally occupy only one location
Source Citation: but some may occupy multiple locations (2x2 area) due to enormous size.

Rule Text: Oversized units take damage equal to the sum of the damage dealt on all of the locations they occupy.
When an oversized unit moves
Source Citation: you choose a direction and all parts of it move in that direction.

Rule Text: If any part of an oversized unit can’t move in the chosen direction
Source Citation: then it can’t move in that direction at all.

Rule Text: When attacking
Source Citation: oversized units still target only a single enemy or site.

Rule Text: A projectile’s flight begins at the shooting unit’s location and travels within the same region in a cardinal direction.
Flight continues until it either reaches the edge of its region or it reaches the first unit (enemy or ally) along its path.
Projectiles ignore any allies at the projectile’s starting location.
In the event of multiple valid units
Source Citation: the player that fired the projectile chooses which unit the projectile hits.

Rule Text: A Storyline begins each time a unit casts a spell or activates an ability.
One or more events are placed at the relevant points on the Storyline and are then resolved in order
Source Citation: one at a time.

Rule Text: Target units or locations must be chosen as an event is being added to the Storyline.
If the resolution of one event triggers an ability
Source Citation: a new event will be inserted into the storyline

Rule Text: If multiple events are competing to be added at the same point on the Storyline
Source Citation: the non-active player places their events first
column4: then the active player places their events.

Rule Text: If a card leaves the realm
Source Citation: any of its events stay on the Storyline but are unable to resolve.

Rule Text: If any event is unable to resolve when it comes up
Source Citation: simply ignore it and continue to the next event.

Rule Text: An Ally/Allied unit is a unit that you control (including your Avatar).
An Enemy is a unit (including Avatars) that is controlled by an opponent.
Banish means to remove from the game.
When a card is conjured or summoned
Source Citation: put it directly into play.

Rule Text: When a card dies
Source Citation: is destroyed
column4: or is killed place it in the cemetery.

Rule Text: While disabled
Source Citation: a minion loses all abilities (including basic abilities like Move and Attack)
column6: and can’t take any action granted by another card or effect.

Rule Text: A disabled minion can still be forcibly moved or tapped by effects.
An Immobile unit can’t take steps.
A unit interacts with the realm if it strikes
Source Citation: deals damage

Rule Text: Any strictly positive amount of damage this card deals to a minion is enough to kill.
Sacrificing is removing something you control from the realm and placing it directly in its owner’s cemetery.
A player may be forced to sacrifice something if an effect instructs them to do so.
When a minion is sacrificed
Source Citation: it does indeed die

Rule Text: When a site is sacrificed
Source Citation: it is replaced by rubble.

Rule Text: A dying unit cannot be sacrificed.
If something is sacrificed to pay the cost of an ability
Source Citation: it is not placed in the cemetery until after that ability has resolved.

Rule Text: A card that is silenced loses all printed and granted abilities.
Avatars cannot be silenced.
When a card is transformed it is considered the same game object as its previous version and therefore does not suffer from summoning sickness when it comes into play.
